name: Gronw Pebr
description: Gronw Pebr, whose name possibly translates to 'Gronw the Radiant' or 'Gronw the Bright,' is a figure primarily known from the Fourth Branch of the Mabinogi, the tale of Math fab Mathonwy. His significance lies not in widespread worship or a vast array of myths, but rather in his crucial role within this specific narrative. He is portrayed as a skilled hunter and lover, characterized by both cunning and a willingness to act decisively, even ruthlessly, to achieve his desires. Gronw is a prominent character in the story of Lleu Llaw Gyffes's fate. His actions highlight themes of betrayal, love triangles, and the intricate web of fate that often ensnares characters in Celtic mythology. While not a major deity in the pantheon sense, Gronw Pebr exemplifies a specific archetype found in many Celtic tales: the powerful figure who disrupts the established order through passionate action. His demise at the hands of Lleu serves as a cautionary tale, illustrating the consequences of treachery and the ultimate triumph of justice within the framework of Celtic heroic narratives.
subtitle: The Radiant Hunter

keyMyths.title: The Seduction of Aranrhod
keyMyths.description: Gronw Pebr, while hunting in the lands of Math fab Mathonwy, encounters the beautiful Aranrhod, Lleu Llaw Gyffes's mother. He becomes infatuated with her, leading to a complex situation that ultimately involves magic and deception. Gronw's passion for Aranrhod sets in motion a chain of events that significantly impacts the lives of Lleu and Gwydion, demonstrating the disruptive power of desire within the established social order. This event reveals Gronw's bold and impulsive nature.
keyMyths.source: The Mabinogion, Fourth Branch: Math fab Mathonwy

keyMyths.title: The Plot Against Lleu Llaw Gyffes
keyMyths.description: Consumed by his love for Aranrhod, Gronw conspires with her to learn the specific conditions under which Lleu Llaw Gyffes can be killed. Aranrhod, through trickery, reveals to Lleu the seemingly impossible circumstances. Gronw then uses this knowledge to attempt to murder Lleu. This act of treachery highlights Gronw's ruthless determination and his willingness to exploit vulnerabilities in order to achieve his goals, showcasing his role as an antagonist within the narrative. He uses a spear he had been preparing for a year and a day.
keyMyths.source: The Mabinogion, Fourth Branch: Math fab Mathonwy

keyMyths.title: Gronw's Death at the Stone of Goronwy
keyMyths.description: After Lleu Llaw Gyffes recovers from Gronw's attack, he seeks revenge. Lleu confronts Gronw and offers him a chance to defend himself. Gronw accepts, knowing he cannot escape his fate. Lleu throws a spear with such force that it pierces Gronw through the stone slab where he stood, known as the Stone of Goronwy. This event marks the culmination of the conflict between the two men and the restoration of order. The Stone of Goronwy still exists today, with the hole in it from Lleu's spear.
keyMyths.source: The Mabinogion, Fourth Branch: Math fab Mathonwy
